Otherwise, the next person is not necessarily a speaker per se, but Lyndon Mum is someone who I've come into contact with from the internet world and the gym world. And he's one of the few people that has just a kind of pristine reputation. He's kind of an old school business guy who has, he's built his business brick by brick for a quarter of a century now, so 25 years in. He's a rubber and flooring manufacturer. And so he does, he's the guy who has the rubber who sells to retailers who then sell to gym owners. And you guys would all shoot me. But I'm very passionate about it. So the answer is no. I worked hard on that slide. So it's not the same. It all depends on the raw materials. I can go in this forever, but the binder is everything. It can be made. We can make stuff that looks just the same, but so much cheaper. We've had floors that have lasted over 25 years. Does not crack or deteriorate. Does not stink. Easy to install. And we stand behind the product of the Airbiz manufacturing thing. Made in America. Does not contain harmful fillers. If any of you saw the lumber liquidators thing where they're putting formaldehyde in the laminate, and the mothers got mad about that because their babies were crawling on formaldehyde. So some of the stuff that's coming from overseas has some bad, bad stuff in there. And we also reguipoles the ones that invented recycled rubber. So this is just kind of the same thing that Alex just did. This is because there's not enough proper binder in there. I'm just gonna rip through these pretty quick here. Villanova, you see how cool, how nice, because our material cuts so nicely. See how nicely we can get the transition, the seams there? It was cheap rubber. You would never see that type of a seam. Those logos are all waterjet cut in. That's a sticker because that one's wood. Like I said, that's a big waterjet cut in. It's all made in rubber. We stack it on a waterjet machine, put the different colors in. It's a CAD-CAM machine and the installers piece it back together. So this is really for collegiate, for high schools, really popular Notre Dame. Most people have heard of them. Another logo. That's their equipment room. And that's actually Regapol Spokeman, who's saying bold for the track side of the business. Tracks are a little bit different than gyms. For every track we sell, we sell probably 15,000 gyms because they're just not as much. Hotels, this is a chain we're working with where they're bringing functional fitness to the, trying to make the hotel gym more exciting. That's a program that's gonna be running on. And CrossFit in 2008, 2008 or late 2000, so we met with CrossFit. They had well under a thousand boxes at the time. Got home that night, my wife said how the meeting go and I said, those guys are crazy, they'll never make it. So I like telling that story because it reminds me to never assume something. So we've been with them since late 2007. We do, we are the official rubber and the turf of the CrossFit Games. We do all the regionals, we do the invitations, which is always someplace and let's see in 16, it was in Spain last year, I think it was in Toronto. We do the finals, we'll be in Madison in August. We provide all the rubber and turf. This was 2009, the first one we did about Dave Castro's family's ranch in Aromas, California. And we just loose-layed the rubber and the big question was would it hold up? Everybody was using stall mats at the time. So the big question was to hold up on Tuesday or Monday afterwards, we went in and checked everything. There was not a ding, a dent, it was dirty, but they resold that. This was 2010 when it moved to Carson, 2011, 12. And what they've done, you see here by his foot, they can have a surface down and then they can lay out the different pieces of rubber. We have different colored rubber and they put stickers on there with the numbers and everything so they can, between the events, they can change things up. 15, you can see how you can see the seams there on the rubber, so that's on a flat surface. And then this last year we moved to Madison, they moved, not we, they moved to Madison, Wisconsin. We have 17,000 square feet of two-by-two interlocking tiles, which is the blue part, and then the rubber is on top, which they just roll up. This was the turf we provided for the games last year, and this year we've got quite a bit more going over there, it's being manufactured right now. I was pushing the wrong button. Yeah. Yeah. Pretty cool, huh? So, what's cool about that though, is because what you guys are learning with Alex, you're gonna have gyms nicer than that. So, what the buying program is, Alex contacted me and created basically a buyer program to kinda give you guys more value, allows the group to buy his one, and he mentioned it early, this is the only group that we work with that has asked for anything back, almost all of them, snap, all those guys, anytime we have to pay a percentage in order to become a preferred vendor with them. So, what happens is, they force their franchisees to buy from their preferred vendors, and then they get a percentage back, it's just how it's always been done. Alex is the first one who hasn't done that, which means you guys save even more money. So, I was surprised when one of our, he loves you guys. I was really surprised on our conversation on the phone, because I said, what do you want, give me your percentage, he goes, I don't want anything, I want dad value, and I thought, cool. So, works for me. So, let's see. So, there is one catch though, see the fine print? Oh, by the way, that video, my 17 year old son did that, so I'm kinda, he's pretty talented. The one catch, because we're just asking that you don't share this prices with the public, we have dealers all across the country, we have big and small, and if you send my quote to you to one of the dealers that's paying the same or a little bit more, then they start coming back to us, and they start complaining. And so, we just ask you guys, just, you know, and if you really go out and shop, you have to compare apples to apples, you might be able to get some stuff from China or somebody that's for 10 cents a square foot cheaper, you're not gonna get it for a dollar cheaper, maybe 10 cents, it's gonna last you for two years, versus something that's gonna pay for 20 years. So, that's our big thing that we ask from you, that you just don't share it. If we start getting too many dealer complaints, we'll just have to discontinue offer, which I'm sure is not gonna happen. Just because, you know, most of the time, I'm not gonna be doing anything, most guys don't have the integrity that Lyndon does, the reason he's been in the business for 25 years is, it's all about reputation, and he knows that, it's a small world, just like it is in the gym world, so he knows this world really well. He, I think, despite the fact, he put a whole bunch of big names up there, but I think he's been one of the people that's strategically pivoted, to catering to more mom and pop, I'm saying, like us gym owners, and so he, you know, in our conversations, he was like, I prefer, I'd rather have 10, you know, 2000 square foot gyms than one 20,000 square foot any day of the week, and so for me, that's kind of what I'm looking for, and obviously with his track record, like he's the best in the industry, and I didn't know this, but I remember sending him a quote that I had gotten, this was like a couple years ago, from one of the, it was like, shoot, I can't remember, it was like coast to coast, whatever, it was one of the Orange County, like the rubber dealer's the guy that I used to get all my stuff from, he's like, yeah, I sell him the stuff that he's selling you, and I was like, got it, all right, so I'm gonna go to you now.
